Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal: The objective is to create an Accepting Petri Net for a specific insurance claim management process using Python and the pm4py library. I need to define places, transitions, arcs, an initial marking, and a final marking based on the provided scenario.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Scenario: Start: Customer files an Insurance Claim (C). Step 1: Claim Registration (CR). Step 2: Document Verification (DV). Branch 1 (Loop): If DV fails (incomplete docs) -> Re-Document Submission (RDS) -> Back to DV. Key Logic: Start -> CR -> DV. DV -> (Success) -> FC OR (Failure) -> RDS -> DV.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Branch 2 (Loop): If FC raises doubts -> In-depth Investigation (II) -> Back to FC. (Note: The prompt says This can happen multiple times, implying a loop).

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Refinement on AP: The prompt says The claim must be approved... if the amount surpasses a certain threshold. In a Petri net, this is usually modeled as an optional path... To keep it deterministic... I will treat AP as a transition that follows CA.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Refinement on Example Pattern: The example shows: 1. Create Net. 2. Create Places (source, sink). 3. Create Transitions. 4. Add Places/Transitions to Net. 5. Add Arcs. 6. Create Markings (im, fm).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis: The prompt example might be demonstrating a specific way to handle the End state... Better approach: I will create a Source and a Sink. I will define im on Source. I will define fm on Sink. I will not remove the sink place.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Constraint Checklist & Confidence Score: 1. Use pm4py.objects.petri_net.obj.PetriNet? Yes. 2. Use Marking? Yes. 3. Use petri_utils? Yes. 4. Follow the scenario? Yes. 5. Follow the example code style? Yes.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self-Correction: I will follow the example's pattern of having a Source and Sink, but I will not delete the Sink place in the final output because fm needs to reference a place in net.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Refining the Logic (AP Step): In a Petri net without data, we cant check thresholds. We have two options: 1. Make AP optional (XOR split). 2. Make AP mandatory (Sequence). Given the list is 1-8... I will treat it as a sequence step.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review against Constraints: Use pm4py.objects.petri_net.obj.PetriNet. Use pm4py.objects.petri_net.obj.Marking. Use pm4py.objects.petri_net.utils.petri_utils. Include loops for DV/RDS and FC/II. Include all steps. Define im and fm.
